instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Additional words: 1025
Know the List of Medical Tests Required for Term Insurance:
Term insurance plans provide coverage against premature death. As such, before the insurance company issues the plan, it assesses the mortality risk of the individual. This risk is determined by your health. As such, the insurer assesses your health to determine whether you are healthy or not. So, if you opt for a very high sum assured, if your age is high, or if you have declared an adverse medical condition the insurance company would ask you to undergo a list of medical tests to assess your health.
What Are the Medical Tests Needed for Term Insurance?
The medical tests needed for term insurance depend on the insurance company. However, some of the common tests that all insurers use include the following –
Why Are Medical Tests Needed?
Insurance underwriters, those who analyse and evaluate the risks of insuring individuals and groups, assess the risks of providing term insurance coverage to you based on your age, health and lifestyle habits. To ascertain the state of your health, the life insurance company has to carry out medical tests and quantify the extent of risk they are carrying by agreeing to provide you with term insurance protection.
Medical test for term insurance is subject to the discretion of the insurance company.
. A medical test also ensures that you don’t have any undisclosed medical condition that you have not declared or are not aware of that may lead to claim rejection in the future. Though the medical tests may report  your current health and your predisposition to some diseases and in turn may affect your term insurance premium, it’s always safer than carrying the risk of claim rejection. Therefore, undergoing medical tests before buying term insurance creates a win-win situation for both the insurer and the insured.
Factors Affected by Term Insurance Medical Test
Depending on the findings of the medical tests conducted, the insurance company might propose a change in the original insurance proposal. The following factors can be impacted by the findings of the medical tests –
Premium payable
If any adverse medical condition is discovered, the insurance company may revise the premium payable for the policy. This is called premium loading, and it is done to compensate the insurance company for the high level of mortality risk that it underwrites by issuing the life insurance policy.
Sum assured
The insurance company might restrict the sum assured allowed under the policy if there is any medical condition that enhances the mortality risk. This would allow the insurer to reduce its risk exposure.
Claim rejection
The possibility of claim rejection can be minimized since the insurance company can see any adverse medical condition that you might have.
Policy issuance
If the insurance company finds severe medical complications that increase the mortality risk considerably, it might not underwrite such risks. In such cases, the insurer might reject the proposal and not issue the coverage at all.
How is Term Insurance Medical Test Conducted for BALIC?
Like other insurers, BALIC too will require tele-medical of physical tests to ascertain your risk profile.
In case of physical tests, you may have to fix an appointment at one of the networked laboratories of the insurance company. There, you can get a medical test done and the costs may be borne by the insurer.
After the tests are done, the reports are shared with the insurance company. Once BALIC underwrites your proposal, the policy is issued if your test reports do not indicate any major medical complications.
Undergo the Term Insurance Medical Tests to Get the Ideal Coverage
When buying a term insurance policy, don’t get scared by the requirement of medical tests. Undergo the tests required so that you become eligible to opt for the requiredsum assured, and you get the coverage for your financial security.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789

output.final_text:
Know the List of Medical Tests Required for Term Insurance
Term insurance plans provide coverage against premature death. As such, before the insurance company issues the plan, it assesses the mortality risk of the individual. This risk is determined by your health condition. As such, the insurer assesses your health to determine whether you are healthy or not. So, under various scenarios the insurance company would ask you to undergo a list of medical tests to assess your health, for eg; if you opt for a very high sum assured, if your age is high, or if you have declared an adverse medical condition in proposal form.
What Are the Medical Tests Needed for Term Insurance?
The medical tests needed for term insurance depend on the insurance company and the medical condition/disclosure provided by the proposer. However, some of the common tests that all insurers use include the following –
Medical tests Meaning BMI tests. The BMI test measures your height and weight to calculate your Body Mass Index (BMI). This helps the insurer assess whether you are underweight, overweight or have a normal weight. Urine Test The Urine Analysis Test is conducted to check your urine and find if you have high blood glucose levels and other ailments. Complete Blood Count (CBC) This test counts your blood cells, i.e., red blood cells and white blood cells. This helps the insurer ensure that your blood work is normal and that you don’t suffer from any hidden ailment Blood Sugar Diabetes is a major condition that impairs health. That is why blood sugar levels are an important metric that insurers check to find out whether you are diabetic and if you do, the type of diabetes that you suffer from Kidney Function Test The Kidney function test helps the insurer check the functionality and normalcy of your kidneys Liver Function Test The Liver function test checks the health of the liver to find out if there are any issues or not Lipid Profile The Lipid profile checks your blood cholesterol levels to rule out any problems and their effects on your body TMT TMT checks your heart health and functioning. It checks your heart’s capacity at intense stress levels Chest X-Ray. A Chest X-Ray gives insight into the working of your heart, lungs and rib cage. In the case of minor health risks involving blood vessels, the X-Ray can help HIV Test. This test is done to check if the insured is suffering from HIV/AIDS Ultrasonography Ultrasonography checks the internal organs, usually the digestive and reproductive systems. It tells whether the organs are working effectively or not.
Echocardiography Echocardiography checks your heart health and functioning. It involves various tests like the 3D and stress echocardiography.
Why Are Medical Tests Needed?
Insurance underwriters, those who analyze and evaluate the risks of insuring individuals and groups, assess the risks of providing term insurance coverage to you based on your age, health and lifestyle habits and other health related disclosure made by the proposer. To ascertain the state of your health, the life insurance company has to carry out medical tests and quantify the extent of risk they are carrying by agreeing to provide you with term insurance protection.
Medical test for term insurance is subject to the discretion of the insurance company.
A medical test also ensures that you don’t have any undisclosed medical condition that you have not declared or are not aware of, that may lead to claim rejection in the future. Though the medical tests may report your current health and your predisposition to some diseases and in turn may affect your term insurance premium, it’s always safer than carrying the risk of claim rejection. Therefore, undergoing medical tests before buying term insurance creates a win-win situation for both the insurer and the insured.
Factors Affected by Term Insurance Medical Test
Depending on the findings of the medical tests conducted, the insurance company might propose a change in the original insurance proposal. The following factors can be impacted by the findings of the medical tests –
● Premium payable If any adverse medical condition is discovered, the insurance company may revise the premium charged for the policy. This is called premium loading, and it is done to compensate the insurance company for the high level of mortality risk that it underwrites by issuing the life insurance policy. ● Sum assured The insurance company might restrict the sum assured allowed under the policy if there is any medical condition that enhances the mortality risk. This would allow the insurer to reduce its risk exposure. ● Claim rejection The possibility of claim rejection can be minimized since the insurance company would know about any adverse medical condition that you might have, prior to issuing policy and assuming the risk. ● Policy issuance If the insurance company finds severe medical complications that increase the mortality risk considerably, it might not underwrite such risks. In such cases, the insurer might not issue the coverage at all.
How is Term Insurance Medical Test Conducted for Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company (BALIC)?
Similar to other life insurers, BALIC too will require tele-medical or physical tests to ascertain your risk profile.
In case of physical tests, you may have to fix an appointment at one of the networked laboratories of BALIC. There, you can get a medical test done.
After the tests are done, the reports are shared with the insurance company. Once BALIC underwrites your proposal basis the medical examination report, the policy is issued.
Undergo the Term Insurance Medical Tests to Get the suitable Coverage
When buying a term insurance policy, don’t get scared by the requirement of medical tests. Undergo the tests required so that you become eligible to opt for the required sum assured, and you get the coverage for your financial security.
Disclaimer: The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
